articles.title: YOUR AI AGENT DEMO LOOKED GREAT. NOW SHIP IT.
articles.summary: The hard part of building AI agents isn't getting an LLM to respond — it's getting it to respond _correctly_, from _your_ data, in production.

articles.title: Download the ebook
NANO BANANA 2
articles.summary: Google's Nano Banana 2 (Gemini 3.1 Flash Image) combines the advanced reasoning and image quality of Nano Banana Pro with significantly faster generation speeds.

articles.title: STATEMENT FROM DARIO AMODEI ON OUR DISCUSSIONS WITH THE DEPARTMENT OF WAR
articles.summary: Anthropic believes in the importance of using AI to defend the US and other democracies and to defeat its autocratic adversaries. This is why it has worked proactively to deploy its models to the Department of War and the intelligence community. However, it also acknowledges that, in some cases, AI can undermine, rather than defend, democratic values. Two such use cases are mass domestic surveillance and full autonomous weapons. Anthropic has refused to accede to the Department of War's request to remove safeguards in its models. It will help the Department transition to another provider to avoid any disruption to ongoing military planning should it decide to offboard Anthropic.

articles.title: XAI CO-FOUNDER TOBY POHLEN IS LATEST EXECUTIVE TO DEPART
articles.summary: xAI co-founder Toby Pohlen is leaving the startup. Pohlen was in charge of Macrohard, a division of the company focused on AI software run by agents. He is the seventh of 12 xAI co-founders to leave in less than three years. xAI merged with SpaceX earlier this month. SpaceX plans to go public in what is expected to be the largest initial public offering of all time.

articles.title: HYPERSCALER CAPEX HAS QUADRUPLED SINCE GPT-4'S RELEASE, NEARING HALF A TRILLION DOLLARS IN 2025
articles.summary: Hyperscaler capital expenditures have grown 70% per year since the release of GPT-4. Company statements and analyst projections anticipate continued rapid spending growth in capital expenditures in 2026. Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, Microsoft, and Oracle are predicted to spend a collective $770 billion on capex this year. There was nearly half a trillion dollars invested in AI in total last year.

articles.title: NEXT-TOKEN PREDICTOR IS AN AI'S JOB, NOT ITS SPECIES
articles.summary: Overemphasizing next-token prediction is a confusion of levels. On the levels where AI is a next-token predictor, humans are also next-token predictors. On the levels where humans are not next-token predictors, AI isn't one either. While AI was shaped by next-token prediction, the inside of its thoughts probably looks like a world-model, the same as a human's.

articles.title: BUILDING INTERACTIVE WORLDS
articles.summary: Real‑time interactive environments require tight integration between simulation engines, agent AI, and live user input to create dynamic, persistent worlds. State management, sensory abstraction, behavior logic, and rendering pipelines must work in concert to support scalable multi‑user interaction. Predictable agent behavior and efficient event propagation are core requirements for responsive, interactive experiences.

articles.title: LLMS DON'T SHIP BAD CODE. TEAMS DO
articles.summary: When AI codes, who's accountable for quality? Today, AI assistants generate Playwright tests, AI healers patch broken selectors, and everything stays green, even when tests don't preserve business intent. mabl [11] calls this “logic drift,” and it gets worse with

articles.title: scale. Read the full breakdown on mabl's blog
REALTIME PROMPTING GUIDE
articles.summary: OpenAI announced the general availability of the Realtime API alongside gpt-realtime, a speech-to-speech model with improved instruction following, tool use, voice quality, and lower latency. The accompanying prompting guide outlines a practical prompt structure and techniques tailored specifically for realtime voice systems.

articles.title: INTRODUCING HELM
articles.summary: helm is a TypeScript framework for AI agents. It allows agents to call typed functions with structured inputs and outputs. helm ships with built-in skills for everyday actions, and users can define custom skills. Its 'execute' tool runs whatever JavaScript the LLM writes within a sandbox.

articles.title: DUALPATH FOR HIGH-THROUGHPUT AGENTIC LLM INFERENCE
articles.summary: DualPath introduces a dual-path KV-cache loading strategy that enables both storage-to-prefill and storage-to-decode transfers, alleviating I/O bottlenecks in disaggregated inference systems.

articles.title: PERPLEXITY APIS POWER ANDROID OEM AI FEATURES
articles.summary: Perplexity is supplying its AI APIs to a major Android device maker to enhance on‑device and cloud‑connected features. The integration brings Perplexity's deep research, summarization, and knowledge retrieval capabilities directly into the handset ecosystem at scale. This move accelerates manufacturer differentiation and broadens Perplexity's footprint in consumer‑facing AI experiences.

articles.title: ON-DEVICE FUNCTION CALLING IN GOOGLE AI EDGE GALLERY
articles.summary: Google's on-device AI showcase app, Google AI Edge Gallery, is now available on iOS in addition to Android. It includes two out-of-the-box agentic experiences, Mobile Actions and Tiny Garden, which showcase how Google's efficient FunctionGemma model translates natural language directly into function calls on devices within merely 270M parameters. Benchmarking has now been integrated directly into the Google AI Edge Gallery app, so users can measure and experience LiteRT's leading CPU and GPU performance across their devices.

articles.title: HOW TO VIBE CODE AGENTIC NETWORKS YOU CAN ACTUALLY DEPLOY
articles.summary: Cognizant's AI Lab explains how to quickly prototype, test, and ground multi-agent systems at scale using neuro-san, an open-source

articles.title: framework for agentifying your enterprise. Read the article
VIBE CODING AND THE MAKER MOVEMENT
articles.summary: Vibe coding differs from previous tech movements by skipping the experimental "scenius" phase, leading to rapid, judgment-free outputs and an evaluative disconnect.

articles.title: HOW AI IS REVOLUTIONIZING HIRING IN COMPETITIVE TALENT MARKETS
articles.summary: AI transforms hiring by efficiently screening candidates and predicting long-term success, improving both speed and accuracy in recruitment.

articles.title: AI WELFARE AS A DEMOTIVATOR FOR TAKEOVER
articles.summary: Superhuman AI might be deterred from takeover by being incentivized to avoid risk, considering factors like the probability of getting caught, the desirability of a non-takeover state, and its risk tolerance.
